name: spymarine

metadata.name: spymarine
metadata.summary: Communicate with Simarine devices using asyncio and Python

documentation:
# spymarine

A library for spying on Simarine devices and their sensor values using asyncio and Python

Based on the fantastic reverse engineering work of https://github.com/htool/pico2signalk

Only tested with Simarine Pico rev2 and firmware 1.17

## Library Installation

```sh
pip install spymarine
```

## Getting Started

Make sure your Simarine device is configured to use AP or STA Wifi mode. It's not possible
to connect via a local network while in REMOTE mode.

Run the following code on the same network that the Simarine device is connected to:

```python
import asyncio
import spymarine

async def main():
    # Print all devices and their latest sensor values every second
    async with spymarine.DeviceReader() as reader:
        while True:
            await reader.read_sensors()
            print(reader.devices)
            await asyncio.sleep(1)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())
```

## Troubleshooting

#### Q: `DeviceReader` is stuck while establishing a connection when using STA Wifi mode

- Update the Pico's firmware

## Known Issues

- Non-ASCII characters in device names will not be represented correctly and replaced with a
  placeholder. A non-standard encoding seems to be used.

## Author

Christopher Strack

classes.name: spymarine.device_reader.DeviceReader

methods.name: spymarine.device_reader.DeviceReader.__init__
documentation: Creates a new device reader

methods.name: spymarine.device_reader.DeviceReader.open
documentation:
Opens the communication with a Simarine device and requests all device
information or loads them from a file.

methods.name: spymarine.device_reader.DeviceReader.close
documentation: Closes an open communication

methods.name: spymarine.device_reader.DeviceReader.read_sensors
documentation: Waits for a broadcast message and updates all sensor values

Read the current state for devices in a Simarine setup.
It has only been tested with the Simarine Pico rev2.

Note that only one device can be connected at the same time including the Simarine app.

open() will connect and read all device information. Afterwards devices are accessible
via the devices property.

read_sensors() will wait for a state update message and update the sensor values of all
devices.

classes.name: spymarine.sensor.Sensor

methods.name: spymarine.sensor.Sensor.update
documentation: Update the sensor value based on a PropertyDict from a state update

methods.name: spymarine.sensor.Sensor.convert
documentation: Converts the given PropertyValue to the sensor value in the defined unit

methods.name: spymarine.sensor.Sensor.asdict
documentation:
Convert the sensor to a dict leaving out internal properties. Useful for
converting a sensor to JSON.

class_variables.name: spymarine.sensor.Sensor.UNIT
documentation: A string describing the unit of the value property for this sensor

class_variables.name: spymarine.sensor.Sensor.sensor_id
documentation: A virtual unique identifier of the sensor

class_variables.name: spymarine.sensor.Sensor.value
documentation: The current value of the sensor

class_variables.name: spymarine.sensor.Sensor.state_index
documentation: The absolute index in a state PropertyDict

class_variables.name: spymarine.sensor.Sensor.state_offset
documentation:
The offset of this sensor's state in a PropertyDict relative to the first
sensor in a device. Used to define the sensor inside a device.
Represents a sensor of a device

functions.name: spymarine.device.device_from_property_dict
documentation: Converts a PropertyDict describing a device into the corresponding Device object

classes.name: spymarine.device.Device

methods.name: spymarine.device.Device.sensors
documentation: Iterator for all sensors and their property name of this device

methods.name: spymarine.device.Device.update_sensors
documentation:
Update all sensors for this device. Should be called when a state update
has been received.

methods.name: spymarine.device.Device.asdict
documentation:
Convert the device to a dict leaving out internal properties. Useful for
converting a device to JSON.

methods.name: spymarine.device.Device.initialize_sensor_indices
documentation:
Needs to be called after a device has been constructed to initialize
all its sensors. This is cecessary for processing state updates.

class_variables.name: spymarine.device.Device.TYPE
documentation: String describing the type of device

class_variables.name: spymarine.device.Device.SENSOR_INDEX_OFFSET
documentation:
The size of the device inside a state update. Defines how many
values have to be skipped to get to the next device.

class_variables.name: spymarine.device.Device.device_id
documentation: A unique identifier of the device as provided by the Simarine device

class_variables.name: spymarine.device.Device.name
documentation: The name of the device as configured by the user

Represents a device in a Simarine setup.

A device has a name, static properties describing the device and one or more sensors
that are regularly updated.

functions.name: spymarine.property_dict.parse_property_dict
documentation:
Converts bytes received by a Simarine devices to a PropertyDict.
Raises ParsingError in case the given bytes do not contain a valid
PropertyDict.

classes.name: spymarine.property_dict.PropertyValue

methods.name: spymarine.property_dict.PropertyValue.first
documentation: Returns the 2 first bytes as a number

methods.name: spymarine.property_dict.PropertyValue.second
documentation: Returns the 2 last bytes as a number

methods.name: spymarine.property_dict.PropertyValue.number
documentation: Returns all 4 bytes as a number

class_variables.name: spymarine.property_dict.PropertyValue.data
documentation: Raw bytes representing the value

Value in a PropertyDict that can either represent two independent 2 byte numbers
or a single 4 byte number

classes.name: spymarine.property_dict.PropertyDict
documentation: Intermediate representation of certain Simarine responses

functions.name: spymarine.communication.crc
documentation:
Calculate a CRC as accepted by Simarine devices.

Original source: https://github.com/htool/pico2signalk
Copyright Erik Bosman / @brainsmoke

functions.name: spymarine.communication.parse_header
documentation:
Parses the given bytes. Returns a Header on success.
Raises ParsingError if the given bytes are not a valid header.
Note: The header is not fully understood and might not work on
all Simarine devices.

functions.name: spymarine.communication.parse_response
documentation:
Parse a response from a Simarine device. Returns a Message on success.
Raises ParsingError if the given data is not a valid or known Simarine Message.

functions.name: spymarine.communication.make_request
documentation: Returns a valid Simarine request for the given Message.

functions.name: spymarine.communication.make_device_request
documentation:
Returns a device request for the given Device ID. The Simarine device
will reply with a device response that contains the description of the
device.

classes.name: spymarine.communication.MessageType

class_variables.name: spymarine.communication.MessageType.DEVICE_COUNT
documentation: Request the number of connected devices

class_variables.name: spymarine.communication.MessageType.DEVICE_INFO
documentation: Request information about a device

class_variables.name: spymarine.communication.MessageType.SENSOR_STATE
documentation: Sensor update message (UDP)

The message type described by the header.
The enum only represents the known subset.

classes.name: spymarine.communication.Header
documentation: Describes the header of a message

classes.name: spymarine.communication.Message
documentation: A message as received by a Simarine device

classes.name: spymarine.communication._UdpMessage
documentation: Represents a UDP message. Used to share state between objects.

classes.name: spymarine.communication._UDPServerProtocol
documentation:
Implements a UDP protocol that notifies the given event when a new UDP message
is received and writes it to the given message.

classes.name: spymarine.communication.Communication

methods.name: spymarine.communication.Communication.create_udp_server
documentation: Create an UDP broadcast server that listens to incoming messages

methods.name: spymarine.communication.Communication.discover_ip
documentation: Find the Simarine device by waiting for a UDP broadcast message

methods.name: spymarine.communication.Communication.close
documentation: Closes an open connection

methods.name: spymarine.communication.Communication.request
documentation:
Send a request to a Simarine device over TCP and returns a response
Raises ParsingError in case no valid response has been returned.

methods.name: spymarine.communication.Communication.receive_broadcast
documentation:
Receive the next incoming UDP message
Raises ParsingError in case the next message is invalid.

methods.name: spymarine.communication.Communication._receive_udp
documentation: Waits until the next UDP broadcast message is received and returns it

Communicate with Simarine devices by making requests and listening
for broadcast messages.

Simarine devices communicate via TCP and UDP broadcast messages.
UDP broadcast messages report new sensor values in short intervals and
can be used to discover the device. TCP is used to request specific
information and probably make changes to the device.
